comments.text: hahaha mate, joins the club! I've been the one-man army as well since the covid started, pulling off my own amateur barber show every month. comments.text: oh man, i can relate an unfortunate hair experience. i've had mine cut into what can only be described as 'helmet hair' once. it took a good month to grow back into a more, shall we say, 'human' look. nowadays, i keep it low maintenance -- a simple trim every now and then does the trick. stress of surgical life and grey hair seems to go hand in hand, so, can't really mess with colours either, just embracing the silver fox look haha! lesson learned anyway: sometimes simpler is better.

gender.inference: The comment discusses a personal experience with haircuts, mentioning "helmet hair" and a preference for low-maintenance styles. The author also references the stress of "surgical life" and "embracing the silver fox look," which could imply a professional background in surgery and a mature age. The term "silver fox" is often associated with older men who have grey hair but maintain an attractive appearance. The language used is casual and humorous, with a focus on practicality and acceptance of aging. While hair-related discussions can be common among all genders, the specific mention of "silver fox" and the context of surgical life might suggest a male perspective.
